title: Ship minimum-viable autonomy dashboard (wake reason + pause toggle)
description:
## Source
Council review at `Data/Pages/council/blueprint-yaml-autonomy-council-2026-06-29.md` — Human Learning Advocate finding  
Autonomy design Phase 1 (promoted from Phase 5)

## Background
The autonomy design places dashboard visibility in Phase 5, but operators encountering autonomous behavior before they can inspect or control it will distrust the system. The Human Learning Advocate strongly recommends these features ship alongside (not after) autonomous behavior.

## Scope
### Minimum viable dashboard additions:
1. **Wake reason display** — dashboard header shows current `Wake Reason: Timer | Follow-up: OnGoalComplete | Chain: 2/5`
2. **Suppressed wake counter** — badge showing suppressed wakes in current session
3. **"Pause autonomy" toggle** — two channels:
   - Chat command (`!pause` / `!resume`) — added to deterministic fast-path parser
   - Dashboard button — SignalR push + REST endpoint
4. **Last suppressed wake** — show most recent suppressed wake with reason

### Pause semantics:
- Pause suppresses: TimerDue, ActionCompleted, GoalCompleted signals
- Pause does NOT suppress: ChatReceived, ExternalCommand (operator override)
- Pause state is visible in dashboard and status endpoint

## Deliverables
- Wake reason display in dashboard header
- Suppressed wake counter
- "Pause autonomy" toggle (chat command + dashboard button)
- REST endpoint: GET /api/agent/autonomy-status
- SignalR event: AutonomyStatusChanged

## Exit Criteria
- Operator can see current wake reason at a glance
- Operator can pause/resume autonomous behavior
- Paused state suppresses non-chat signals
- Chat always works during pause
- State visible in dashboard and status endpoint
